message:
perf(vdbe/page-lock): drop full Cx::checkpoint from per-iteration wait loop

The page-lock-holder wait loop in `wait_for_page_lock_holder_change` was
calling `observe_execution_cancellation(cx)` — which runs the full
`Cx::checkpoint` (e-process oracle observation + native cx plane) — on
*every* wait iteration. Each wait slice is already bounded by
`PAGE_LOCK_WAIT_CANCELLATION_POLL` (5 ms), so a slightly-delayed
cancellation signal is still observed promptly on the next iteration
via the outer-caller's checkpoint.

Replaces the per-iteration full checkpoint with a direct
`cx.is_cancel_requested()` atomic-bool load: if the local cancel flag is
set, return `FrankenError::Abort` immediately; otherwise continue the
wait. The outer-loop check at the top of the caller's loop still runs
the full checkpoint, so the e-process oracle path is preserved — we just
don't re-run it inside a 5ms-bounded inner tick.

Numbers: the old per-iteration checkpoint accounted for 1.58% self-time
on the 2026-04-23 MT 8t DWARF capture
(`fsqlite-memmove-dwarf-213322`). Hot contended-lock workloads (MT MVCC
bench, concurrent writers racing for the same page) see the inner loop
spin for many iterations before the holder changes, so this is a
linear-in-wait-count win.

Semantics preserved:
  - Outer `observe_execution_cancellation` call at entry still runs the
    full checkpoint before we start waiting (comment added to make this
    explicit so future refactors don't regress it).
  - Local cancel flag is flipped by all the paths that would previously
    have flipped the checkpoint (see `Cx::request_cancel`), so the
    semantics for *this specific method* are unchanged.
  - If a caller ever becomes the sole consumer of the e-process oracle
    observation inside a long-running single-page wait (no outer loop),
    the bounded 5ms poll still gives the outer checkpoint a chance to
    fire within a controlled latency envelope.

No API change; no test change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
